title: Pleconax grisebachii
taxonomy: Pleconax grisebachii is a species of freshwater snail with gills and an operculum. It belongs to the family Diplommatinidae, which is a family of air-breathing land snails and slugs, but it is itself a freshwater species. Pleconax grisebachii is native to Cuba. It is classified as a terrestrial mollusk that has adapted to live in freshwater environments. This adaptation is a common trait among some species of the family Diplommatinidae.

side_effects: Pleconax grisebachii is a species of snail, and there is limited information available on its potential side effects. However, in general, snails can carry diseases and parasites that can be transmitted to humans, such as rat lungworm. These parasites can cause a range of symptoms, including neurological problems and eosinophilic meningitis. It's essential to handle snails safely and cook them thoroughly before consumption to minimize the risk of disease transmission.
